description: Provide model-readable meanings, units, default effects, and verifier expectations for controllable physical parameters.
physical_assumptions: The model must treat physical parameters as executable controls, not descriptive prose.
Changing one parameter should have a directionally predictable effect when seed, camera, initial state, and action trace are locked.
Inertia, damping, friction, restitution, mass, gravity, and force-field controls must be explicit enough for runtime replay and verifier diagnosis.
verifier_rules: Every physics-critical object declares the parameters required by its capability.
Runtime control echo matches case spec values within tolerance.
Single-parameter sweeps preserve locked camera, action trace, seed, and initial state.
Observed trajectory/contact direction agrees with declared sensitivity direction unless a failure is reported.
repair_suggestions: Move hidden physical assumptions from prompt text into physical_parameters or physics_control.
Add units and expected_effect for each changed parameter.
When a behavior looks wrong, inspect runtime_control_echo before tuning prompts.

parameter_table.name: mass_kg
parameter_table.meaning_for_model: Amount of matter assigned to a rigid body. Higher mass resists acceleration from the same impulse and changes momentum transfer in collisions.
parameter_table.expected_effect: With the same impulse, larger mass produces lower velocity change; in collision, heavier bodies transfer momentum differently from lighter bodies.

parameter_table.name: inertia_scale
parameter_table.meaning_for_model: Multiplier on rotational inertia. Higher inertia_scale makes angular acceleration and spin changes smaller for the same torque or off-center impact.
parameter_table.expected_effect: Increasing inertia_scale slows spin-up and spin-down response without necessarily changing linear motion.

parameter_table.name: linear_velocity_init
parameter_table.meaning_for_model: Initial translational velocity applied at frame 0 only to active or explicitly launched bodies.
parameter_table.expected_effect: Controls starting motion direction and speed; passive targets should usually be [0,0,0] until contact/action/release evidence.

parameter_table.name: angular_velocity_init
parameter_table.meaning_for_model: Initial rotational velocity around object axes.
parameter_table.expected_effect: Creates spin; angular damping should reduce its magnitude over time unless torque is applied.

parameter_table.name: linear_damping
parameter_table.meaning_for_model: Velocity-proportional drag applied to linear motion.
parameter_table.expected_effect: Higher linear_damping shortens travel distance and reduces speed faster.

parameter_table.name: angular_damping
parameter_table.expected_effect: Higher angular_damping makes spin decay faster and prevents unexplained angular energy gain.

parameter_table.name: friction_static
parameter_table.meaning_for_model: Maximum friction ratio that can prevent motion before sliding starts.
parameter_table.expected_effect: Higher static friction increases the threshold required to start sliding.

parameter_table.name: friction_dynamic
parameter_table.expected_effect: Higher dynamic friction reduces sliding speed faster and shortens stop distance.

parameter_table.name: restitution
parameter_table.meaning_for_model: Bounciness or normal velocity retention across impact.
parameter_table.expected_effect: Higher restitution increases rebound height or post-impact separation speed; values above 1 imply energy gain and should be rejected unless explicitly modeled.

parameter_table.name: gravity
parameter_table.meaning_for_model: World acceleration applied to gravity-enabled bodies.
parameter_table.expected_effect: Higher gravity increases downward acceleration, impact speed, and contact timing.

parameter_table.name: collision_profile
parameter_table.meaning_for_model: Runtime collision behavior such as PhysicsActor, BlockAll, NoCollision.
parameter_table.expected_effect: NoCollision bodies should not generate contacts; physics-critical collision bodies must use a collision-enabled profile.

parameter_table.name: constraint_stiffness
parameter_table.meaning_for_model: Resistance of a constraint or spring to displacement.
parameter_table.expected_effect: Higher stiffness reduces stretch but increases rebound force and possible instability if timestep is too large.

parameter_table.name: break_threshold
parameter_table.meaning_for_model: Threshold at which fracture or constraint breaking is allowed.
parameter_table.expected_effect: Break/fracture events should occur only after runtime contact/force exceeds this threshold.
